name: Notion
description: Connect Notion databases as live data sources. Query, create and update entries without duplication.

apiToken.description: Notion internal integration token. Create one at notion.so/profile/integrations/internal

actions.name: Search
actions.description: Search pages and databases by title across the workspace

filter.description: Restrict to pages or data sources

actions.name: Get Page
actions.description: Retrieve a page's content as markdown

actions.name: Create Page
actions.description: Create a page under a parent (page or database) with markdown content

properties.description: Database properties (when parent is a database)

actions.name: Update Page
actions.description: Update a page's markdown content and/or properties

markdown.description: Replace page body with this markdown

actions.name: List Databases
actions.description: List all databases the integration has access to

actions.name: Get Database Schema
actions.description: Retrieve a database's schema (properties/columns with types)

properties.description: Map of property name to {id, type, config}

actions.name: Query Database
actions.description: Query a database with optional filters and sorts

actions.name: Create Database Entry
actions.description: Add a new entry (page) to a database with property values

properties.description: Property values keyed by property name

actions.name: Update Database Entry
actions.description: Update properties of an existing database entry

actions.name: Add Comment
actions.description: Add a comment to a page or discussion

instructions:
Notion integration using an internal integration token (platform secret).

Notion is the single source of truth. Every read goes through the API, every write pushes directly to Notion. No local duplication.

The internal integration only has access to pages and databases explicitly shared with it in Notion (Add Connections).

`search` finds pages and data sources by title. Use `filter: "page"` or `filter: "data_source"` to restrict results.

`list_databases` discovers accessible data sources. `get_database_schema` returns the column structure (property names, types). Use it to understand available fields before querying or creating entries.

`query_database` supports Notion's native filter syntax:
- Text: `{"property": "Name", "rich_text": {"contains": "foo"}}`
- Select: `{"property": "Status", "select": {"equals": "Done"}}`
- Date: `{"property": "Due", "date": {"on_or_after": "2026-01-01"}}`
- Checkbox: `{"property": "Done", "checkbox": {"equals": true}}`
- Compound: `{"and": [{...}, {...}]}` or `{"or": [{...}, {...}]}`

Sorts: `[{"property": "Name", "direction": "ascending"}]` or `[{"timestamp": "last_edited_time", "direction": "descending"}]`

`create_page` and `create_entry` both create pages. The difference is the parent: `create_entry` targets a database (sets properties), `create_page` targets a page (freeform content).

Markdown endpoints (`get_page` returns markdown, `create_page`/`update_page` accept markdown) use Notion's enhanced markdown format.

Property values for `create_entry`/`update_entry` use simplified format (the worker converts to Notion's verbose format):
- Title: `{"Name": "My title"}`
- Rich text: `{"Description": "Some text"}`
- Number: `{"Price": 42.5}`
- Select: `{"Status": "In Progress"}`
- Multi-select: `{"Tags": ["urgent", "frontend"]}`
- Date: `{"Due": "2026-04-15"}` or `{"Due": {"start": "2026-04-15", "end": "2026-04-20"}}`
- Checkbox: `{"Done": true}`
- URL: `{"Link": "https://example.com"}`
- Email: `{"Contact": "a@b.com"}`
- Phone: `{"Phone": "+32 123 456 789"}`
- Status: `{"Status": "In Progress"}`
